quotes.speaker: Jane Ira Bloom
quotes.text: Yeah. That was like a further incarnation of some of that NASA work. Basically that was a piece called “Einstein’s Red/Blue Universe,” and it was played by The American Composers Orchestra, a very fine new music orchestra in New York, conducted by Dennis Russell Davies, and it was my first piece for full symphony orchestra and jazz musicians, and sound surround brass choirs in the Carnegie Hall balconies. It was the funniest thing. We had — in order to maximize the use of the musicians there are points in the score where the brass section has to leave the stage and get up to the balcony in the Carnegie Hall. And that’s pretty precipitous up there by the way. They had to take the elevator to get up there in the middle of the piece.
quotes.caption: Premiere of Einstein’s Red/Blue Universe with ACO
quotes.doc_title: Jane Ira Bloom interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 3, 1998
